title: Triboelectric cells
abstract: This invention relates to a triboelectric cell comprising a first electrode comprising a first material having a first Fermi level, a second electrode comprising a second material having a second Fermi level that is different from the first Fermi level, and a load electrically connecting the first electrode to the second electrode, wherein a contact surface of the first electrode is continuously in contact with a contact surface of the second electrode during operation of thetriboelectric cell and relative movement between the first electrode and the second electrode generates a direct current flow through the load.
claims:
Claims Claims 1. A triboelectric cell comprising: a first electrode comprising a first material having a first Fermi level; a second electrode comprising a second material having a second Fermi level that is different from the first Fermi level; and a load electrically connecting the first electrode to the second electrode, wherein a contact surface of the first electrode is configured to be continuously in contact with a contact surface of the second electrode during operation of the triboelectric cell, and relative movement between the first electrode and the second electrode generates a direct current flow through the load. 2. The triboelectric cell according to claim 1 wherein the second electrode has a smaller dimension than the first electrode. 3. The triboelectric cell according to claim 1 or 2 wherein the first electrode further comprises a first metal material arranged beneath the first material, the second electrode further comprises a second metal material arranged over the second material, and the load is electrically connecting the first metal material to the second metal material. 4. The triboelectric cell according to claim 3 wherein the first material comprises a semiconductor material of a first doping type, and the second material comprises a semiconductor material of a second doping type. 5. The triboelectric cell according to claim 4 wherein the first electrode further comprises another semiconductor material of the second doping type arranged between the first material and the first metal material. 6. The triboelectric cell according to claim 4 or 5 wherein 

 the second electrode further comprises another semiconductor material of the first doping type arranged between the second material and the second metal material. 7. The triboelectric cell according to any one of claims 3 to 6 wherein the first electrode further comprises a first passivation layer arranged on the first material and a first interlayer arranged between the first material and the first metal material, wherein a surface of the first passivation layer facing away from the first material is configured as the contact surface of the first electrode. 8. The triboelectric cell according to any one of claims 3 to 7 wherein the second electrode further comprises a second passivation layer arranged on the second material and a second interlayer arranged between the second material and the second metal material, wherein a surface of the second passivation layer facing away from the second material is configured as the contact surface of the second electrode. 9. The triboelectric cell according to any one of claims 4 to 8 wherein the semiconductor material of the first doping type is an n-type semiconductor material and the semiconductor material of the second doping type is a p-type semiconductor material. 10. The triboelectric cell according to any one of claims 4 to 8 wherein the semiconductor material of the first doping type is a p-type semiconductor material and the semiconductor material of the second doping type is an n-type semiconductor material. 11. The triboelectric cell according to claim 1 or 2 wherein the first material comprises a first metal material, and the second material comprises a second metal material. 12. The triboelectric cell according to claim 11 wherein the first electrode further comprises a first functional layer arranged on the first metal material and configured as the contact surface of the first electrode. 13. The triboelectric cell according to claim 11 or 12 wherein the second electrode further comprises a second functional layer arranged on the second metal material and configured as the contact surface of the second electrode. 14. The triboelectric cell according to any one of claims 11 to 13 wherein the first metal material has a lower work function than the second metal material. 15. The triboelectric cell according to any one of claims 11 to 13 wherein the first metal material has a higher work function than the second metal material. 16. The triboelectric cell according to any one of claims 1 to 15 further comprising: an additive provided between the first electrode and the second electrode. 17. The triboelectric cell according to any one of claims 1 to 16 further comprising: a plurality of the first electrodes, wherein the plurality of the first electrodes are adjacently connected to each other in series; and a plurality of the second electrodes, wherein the plurality of the second electrodes are adjacently connected to each other in series, wherein the load is electrically connecting the last first electrode of the plurality of the first electrodes to the last second electrode of the plurality of the second electrodes. 18. The triboelectric cell according to any one of claims 1 to 16 further comprising: a plurality of a pair of electrodes, wherein each pair of electrodes comprises the first electrode and the second electrode and the plurality of a pair of electrodes are serially connected such that the first electrode of a pair of electrodes is connected to the second electrode of the adjacent right pair of electrodes, the second electrode of the pair of electrodes is connected to the first electrode of the adjacent left pair of electrodes and the load is electrically connected between the second electrode of a first pair of electrodes and the first electrode of a last pair of electrodes. 19. A method for generating a direct current (DC) power based on the triboelectric cell according to any one of the preceding claims comprising: 

 moving the second electrode against the first electrode such that the contact surface of the first electrode is continuously in contact with the contact surface of the second electrode.
